---
title: "Mark Gingrass"
page-layout: full
toc: false
resources:
  - "dark-mode.js"
---

<script src="dark-mode.js"></script>

::: {.hero}
::: {.hero-content}
**Technical Leader • Developer • Problem Solver**

Product Manager • Turning complexity into clarity.
Aligning users, business goals, and engineering to ship solutions that matter.

:::
:::

::: {.container}
<div class="row mt-12">
<div class="col-md-6">

## Professional

Product leader with 20+ years in the federal sector. For the past eight years, I have been specializing in product strategy, user-centered innovation, and delivering solutions with tangible outcomes. I have a track record of defining product vision for complex enterprise systems, translating ambiguous stakeholder needs into actionable roadmaps, and leading cross-functional teams to build products that people actually use and value.

I am the connector and translator across disciplines. I work with user needs, business goals, and technical experts daily. I focus on building trust with stakeholders at every level—from end users to division directors and key decision-makers.
Extensive experience partnering with defense, regulatory, and federal agencies to modernize legacy systems and prove that government technology can be world-class.

### Current Focus

* Leading product strategy for FDA modernization initiatives that serve 400+ regulatory scientists
* Building platforms that reduce manual workflows and accelerate decisions
* Championing AI-powered solutions that unlock decades of trapped regulatory data
* Staying technically credible through hands-on iOS development (SwiftUI, because learning keeps me humble)

</div>
<div class="col-md-6">

![Mark Gingrass Pose](images/MarkPro.webp){.img-fluid .rounded-xl .shadow-lg}

</div>
</div>

<div class="row mt-12">
<div class="col-md-4">

## Outdoor Enthusiast

I've been actively hiking for a few years now. I recently started doing overnighters and love the steep climbs. I also mountain bike and skateboard. I have a partially built-out van and pose as a vanlifer on the weekends from time to time.

My dog, Ollie, is usually along for the ride. He's my trail buddy and road trip companion.

</div>
<div class="col-md-4">

## Curious Mind

I have interests in technology, policy, governance, and efficiency. I enjoy learning about Lean, Six Sigma, and anything related to improving efficiency and eliminating wasteful processes.

At the end of the day, I'm always seeking ways to combine what I know with what I love.

</div>
<div class="col-md-4">

## What I'm Learning

- **AI Agent Workflows**: Building multi-step agentic pipelines with Claude and GPT-4o
- **Regulatory Data Science**: Connecting structured policy data to actionable product insights
- **Prompt Engineering**: Treating prompts like product specs — precise, testable, iterable
- **Political Economy**: Reading deeply across institutions, power structures, and democratic theory

</div>
</div>

## Skills & Technologies

::: {.tech-stack}
**Senior Technology Leader** with expertise spanning federal enterprise modernization, AI-powered product strategy, and human-centered design at scale
:::

<div class="row mt-8">
<div class="col-md-3">

### **Leadership & Strategy**
- **20+ Years** Federal Sector Experience
- **Product Vision** for Complex Enterprise Systems
- **Agile & Lean Transformation** at Scale
- **RFP/RFI Strategy** & Contract Stewardship
- **Stakeholder Alignment** from End User to Executive
- **Six Sigma** Process Optimization

</div>
<div class="col-md-3">

### **Product & Systems Thinking**
- **Outcome-Driven Roadmaps** tied to real user needs
- **Legacy Modernization** in high-compliance environments
- **Human-Centered Design** & Discovery Frameworks
- **API & Platform Thinking** for scalable architecture
- **Static Site & JAMstack** development (Quarto, Git)
- **Data Workflow Automation** & toolchain integration

</div>
<div class="col-md-3">

### **AI & Emerging Technology**
- **Agentic AI Workflows** with Claude & GPT-4o
- **Prompt Engineering** as a product discipline
- **Claude Code & Cursor AI** for accelerated delivery
- **AI-Assisted Documentation** & knowledge capture
- **LLM Evaluation** & responsible deployment thinking
- **Regulatory AI Applications** in federal science domains

</div>
<div class="col-md-4">

### **Communication & Influence**
- **Technical Writing** that non-technical stakeholders actually read
- **Cross-Functional Translation** between engineers, policy leads, and users
- **Public Blog** covering AI, development, and governance
- **Training & Mentorship** of product and technical teams
- **Narrative Strategy** for complex program justification
- **Congressional & Agency-Level** briefing experience

</div>
</div>

## Recent Writing

::: {#recent-posts}
:::

<script>
// Dynamic Recent Posts Loading
document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function() {
  // Recent posts data (this would ideally come from a build-time JSON file)
  const recentPosts = [
    {
      title: "Round Two: Working with AI",
      url: "posts/2025-05-30-round-two-working-with-ai/",
      category: "AI & Technology",
      description: "Reflecting on learning Swift and iOS development while working with AI tools like Cursor, Claude Code, and ChatGPT. Exploring the balance between leveraging AI assistance and maintaining fundamental programming skills.",
      date: "May 30, 2025",
      readTime: "5 min read"
    },
    {
      title: "Working with AI, Habit Tracker - Round Two",
      url: "posts/2025-05-19-simple-habit-tracker-round-two-app/",
      category: "Career & Programming",
      description: "Experimenting with an AI-assisted development workflow for iOS using SwiftUI, Git, and Cursor AI. This post explores my shift toward a Markdown-driven architecture strategy.",
      date: "May 19, 2025",
      readTime: "4 min read"
    },
    {
      title: "Simple Habit Tracker App",
      url: "posts/2025-05-14-simple-habit-tracker-app/",
      category: "Career & Programming",
      description: "Building a custom iOS habit tracker app using SwiftUI and Xcode—with help from large language models to accelerate design, debug Swift code, and iterate features faster.",
      date: "May 14, 2025",
      readTime: "6 min read"
    }
  ];

  // Generate HTML for recent posts
  const recentPostsContainer = document.getElementById('recent-posts');
  if (recentPostsContainer) {
    const blogGrid = document.createElement('div');
    blogGrid.className = 'blog-grid';
    
    recentPosts.forEach(post => {
      const blogCard = document.createElement('div');
      blogCard.className = 'blog-card';
      
      blogCard.innerHTML = `
        <div class="blog-category">${post.category}</div>
        <h3><a href="${post.url}">${post.title}</a></h3>
        <p>${post.description}</p>
        <div class="blog-meta">${post.date} • ${post.readTime}</div>
      `;
      
      blogGrid.appendChild(blogCard);
    });
    
    recentPostsContainer.appendChild(blogGrid);
  }
});
</script>

:::

<script>
// Add scroll animations
document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function() {
  const observerOptions = {
    threshold: 0.1,
    rootMargin: '0px 0px -50px 0px'
  };
  
  const observer = new IntersectionObserver(function(entries) {
    entries.forEach(entry => {
      if (entry.isIntersecting) {
        entry.target.classList.add('animate-fadeInUp');
      }
    });
  }, observerOptions);
  
  // Observe all cards and sections
  document.querySelectorAll('.card, .app-card, .col-md-4, .col-md-6').forEach(el => {
    observer.observe(el);
  });
  
  // Add smooth scrolling for anchor links
  document.querySelectorAll('a[href^="#"]').forEach(anchor => {
    anchor.addEventListener('click', function (e) {
      e.preventDefault();
      const target = document.querySelector(this.getAttribute('href'));
      if (target) {
        target.scrollIntoView({
          behavior: 'smooth',
          block: 'start'
        });
      }
    });
  });
});
</script>
